name: Fedora
description: Fedora is a community-driven RPM-based distribution sponsored by Red Hat, serving as the upstream development branch for Red Hat Enterprise Linux. It ships the latest stable versions of free and open-source software on a six-month release cadence.

name: Fedora Silverblue
description: Fedora Silverblue is an immutable desktop variant of Fedora built on rpm-ostree, where the base system is read-only and updates are applied atomically. It focuses on reliability, container workflows, and Flatpak-based application delivery.

name: Fedora Kinoite
description: Fedora Kinoite is the KDE Plasma counterpart of Fedora Silverblue, offering an immutable rpm-ostree system. It targets users who want a stable KDE desktop with atomic updates and Flatpak applications.

name: Fedora Sericea
description: Fedora Sericea is the Sway tiling-window-manager edition of Fedora Silverblue, providing an immutable base and a Wayland-only Sway desktop. It targets users who prefer a keyboard-driven, minimal Wayland environment.

name: Fedora Onyx
description: Fedora Onyx is the Budgie desktop immutable edition of Fedora, built on the same rpm-ostree base as Silverblue. It offers a modern, clean Budgie experience with atomic updates.

name: Fedora IoT
description: Fedora IoT is the Fedora edition targeted at Internet-of-Things and edge devices, built on rpm-ostree with a focus on container workloads and over-the-air updates. It is a base image for embedded and industrial deployments.

name: Fedora CoreOS
based_on: Fedora Atomic Host + CoreOS Container Linux
description: Fedora CoreOS is an automatically-updating, container-focused operating system derived from the merger of Fedora Atomic Host and CoreOS Container Linux. It is designed for running containerized workloads at scale with minimal manual administration.

name: Fedora Atomic Host
description: Fedora Atomic Host was a container-focused, rpm-ostree based edition of Fedora optimized for running Docker and Kubernetes workloads. It was merged with CoreOS Container Linux in 2020 and fully discontinued with the final release in 2022.

name: Fedora ELN
description: Fedora ELN (Enterprise Linux Next) is a Fedora build that tracks what will become the next major RHEL release, used as a continuous integration and rebuild target. It is not a general-purpose distribution and primarily exists for packagers and CI systems.

name: Fedora Phosh
description: Fedora Phosh is a Fedora edition for mobile and small touch devices, featuring the Phosh shell built on GNOME and Wayland. It supports phones such as the PinePhone Pro and Librem 5.

name: Red Hat Enterprise Linux
release_model: Fixed with long-term support (10 years per major)
description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is Red Hat's commercial Linux distribution aimed at enterprise servers, workstations, and cloud workloads. It is rebuilt from Fedora sources, hardened, and supported for ten years per major release.

name: CentOS Linux
description: CentOS Linux was a free downstream rebuild of Red Hat Enterprise Linux, maintained by the CentOS Project. It was discontinued at the end of 2021 in favor of CentOS Stream as a non-commercial RHEL replacement.

name: CentOS Stream
description: CentOS Stream is a continuously delivered distribution that tracks just ahead of the next RHEL minor release, serving as the upstream development branch for Red Hat Enterprise Linux. It replaced CentOS Linux as the community counterpart to RHEL.

name: Rocky Linux
description: Rocky Linux is a community-driven, binary-compatible rebuild of Red Hat Enterprise Linux created by one of the original CentOS founders after the CentOS shift to Stream. It aims to be a drop-in replacement for RHEL with enterprise-grade stability.

name: AlmaLinux OS
description: AlmaLinux OS is a free, community-driven, binary-compatible rebuild of Red Hat Enterprise Linux maintained by the AlmaLinux OS Foundation. It is supported for many architectures and targets production server and cloud workloads.

name: Oracle Linux
kernel_root: Linux Kernel (RHEL kernel, with Unbreakable Enterprise Kernel option)
description: Oracle Linux is a Red Hat Enterprise Linux rebuild produced by Oracle, intended for Oracle software, database, and cloud workloads. It adds the Unbreakable Enterprise Kernel and the Ksplice zero-downtime kernel patching tool.

name: Scientific Linux
description: Scientific Linux was a Red Hat Enterprise Linux rebuild originally produced at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory and CERN, aimed at scientific computing. It was discontinued in 2019 following the shift in community focus to CentOS Stream and the RHEL rebuilds.

name: EuroLinux
description: EuroLinux is a European RHEL rebuild maintained by the Polish company EuroLinux, offering both free community editions and commercial support subscriptions. It targets enterprises that want a European-hosted RHEL alternative.

name: ClearOS
based_on: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (earlier based on Fedora)
description: ClearOS was a Red Hat-derived small business and network gateway distribution that bundled a web-based admin console for managing users, files, and services. It was discontinued in 2021 after its acquisition and pivot to ContainerPilot.

name: MIRACLE LINUX
description: MIRACLE LINUX is a Japanese enterprise distribution originally built on the Asianux cross-vendor project and now rebuilt from Red Hat Enterprise Linux. It provides Japanese-language enterprise Linux with commercial support from Cybertrust Japan.

name: Navy Linux
description: Navy Linux was a small Chinese RHEL rebuild focused on cloud and server workloads. It was discontinued in 2021 along with several other minor CentOS-style rebuilds after the CentOS Linux transition.

name: OpenELA
description: OpenELA (Open Enterprise Linux Association) is a trade group of competing RHEL rebuilds founded by CIQ, Oracle, and SUSE that publishes enterprise Linux source code and builds. It exists to maintain an open, vendor-neutral base for RHEL-compatible distributions.

name: Virtuozzo Linux
description: Virtuozzo Linux is a RHEL-compatible distribution from Virtuozzo (the company behind OpenVZ) targeting service providers, virtualized infrastructure, and container hosts. It includes the Virtuozzo kernel with container and virtualization enhancements.

name: Springdale Linux
description: Springdale Linux (formerly PUIAS Linux) is a Red Hat Enterprise Linux rebuild maintained by the Institute for Advanced Study and Princeton University, focused on scientific and academic computing. It is binary-compatible with RHEL and freely distributable.

name: XCP-ng
description: XCP-ng is an open-source community fork of Citrix XenServer / Hypervisor, providing a Type-1 hypervisor and turnkey virtualization platform for server infrastructure. It is maintained by Vates, which also develops Xen Orchestra.

name: oVirt
description: oVirt is an open-source virtualization management platform that provides a web-based interface and APIs for managing KVM hosts, virtual machines, storage, and networks. The oVirt Node is a minimal Fedora/RHEL-based hypervisor image.

name: Rocks Cluster Distribution
description: Rocks Cluster Distribution was a CentOS/RHEL-based Linux distribution from the San Diego Supercomputer Center aimed at building and managing HPC compute clusters. It was widely used in academic and research HPC environments before falling behind in active maintenance.

name: Asianux
based_on: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (joint Chinese, Japanese, Korean project)
description: Asianux was a jointly developed RHEL-derived enterprise distribution produced by Red Flag (China), Miracle Linux (Japan), and Haansoft (Korea). It was superseded by each vendor's own distribution after RHEL 8 and is no longer actively maintained as a single product.

name: Nobara
description: Nobara is a Fedora-based distribution tailored for gaming and content creation, with a modified GNOME desktop and out-of-the-box media codecs, drivers, and Steam integration.

name: Ultramarine Linux
description: Ultramarine Linux is a Fedora-based distribution offering a curated desktop experience with multiple desktop editions, Flatpak integration, and support for ARM devices like the Raspberry Pi.

name: Korora Project
description: Korora was a user-friendly Fedora-based distribution offering multiple desktop editions, multimedia codecs, and proprietary drivers enabled by default. It was discontinued in 2017 by its creator Chris Smart.

name: Berry Linux
description: Berry Linux is a Japanese Fedora-based distribution offering Live CD images with a wide variety of desktop environments and Japanese localization. It is maintained as a hobbyist project for Japanese users.

name: Chapeau
description: Chapeau was a Fedora-based distribution focused on delivering a polished desktop experience with multimedia codecs, proprietary drivers, and curated defaults. It was discontinued in 2019 after its lead developer stepped away.

name: Hanthana
description: Hanthana was a Sri Lankan Fedora remix that included Sinhala localization, additional fonts, and media codecs aimed at desktop users in Sri Lanka. It was discontinued after Fedora 28 due to maintainer availability.

name: RebeccaBlackOS
description: RebeccaBlackOS is a Fedora-based distribution that ships a Wayland-only live image focused on demonstrating a complete, working Wayland desktop. It is maintained by developer Drew DeVault's broader Wayland ecosystem team.

name: RisiOS
description: RisiOS was a Fedora-based distribution focused on developer ergonomics and a curated GNOME experience with a range of out-of-the-box tooling. It was discontinued after a short run by its maintainer.

name: Moblin
description: Moblin (Mobile Linux) was an Intel-led Fedora-based distribution targeting netbooks, MIDs, and early mobile devices. It was merged with Nokia's Maemo into MeeGo in 2010, which itself later became Tizen.

name: Linpus Linux
description: Linpus Linux is a Taiwan-based distribution targeting OEM laptops, netbooks, and tablets, with simplified Linpus Lite and Android-derived editions preinstalled on consumer hardware.

name: Red Flag Linux
description: Red Flag Linux was a Chinese Linux distribution developed by Red Flag Software, originating from the Chinese National 863 project and later co-developed into Asianux. Its desktop editions were based on Fedora before being superseded by server-only RHEL-derived builds.

name: VLOS
description: VLOS (Vine Linux Operating System) was an early Fedora-based community distribution targeted at desktop users. It was a short-lived project that did not progress beyond early releases.

name: Pinguy OS
description: Pinguy OS was a user-friendly distribution that began as a Fedora remix and later switched to an Ubuntu base, focused on out-of-the-box desktop usability with codecs and non-free extras enabled. It was discontinued by its maintainer.

name: Universal Blue
release_model: Fixed with rolling updates (follows Fedora base)
description: Universal Blue is a project that produces a family of customized Fedora Silverblue / Kinoite images with curated packages, Flatpaks, and a shared build pipeline. It is the common base from which Bazzite, Bluefin, and Aurora are derived.

name: Bazzite
release_model: Fixed with rolling updates (follows Fedora base)
description: Bazzite is an immutable, Universal Blue-based Fedora image optimized for handheld gaming PCs (Steam Deck, ASUS ROG Ally, Lenovo Legion Go) and console-style living-room use. It bundles Steam, Heroic, Lutris, and gaming-tuned kernel drivers.

name: Bluefin
release_model: Fixed with rolling updates (follows Fedora base)
description: Bluefin is an immutable Universal Blue image built on Fedora Silverblue, positioned as a polished developer workstation with built-in devcontainers, Docker, and pre-tuned defaults. It is the upstream developer-focused counterpart to Aurora.

name: Aurora
release_model: Fixed with rolling updates (follows Fedora base)
description: Aurora is the consumer and creator-oriented Universal Blue image built on Fedora, offering a polished desktop experience for end users on top of an immutable base. It is the non-developer sibling of Bluefin within the Universal Blue family.

name: Aurora DX
release_model: Fixed with rolling updates (follows Fedora base)
description: Aurora DX is the developer edition of Aurora, layering the Bluefin developer toolchain on top of the Aurora desktop experience. It is intended for power users and tinkerers who want a polish plus dev-tooling blend within the Universal Blue family.

name: uCore
description: uCore is a Universal Blue server / container host image built on a Fedora CoreOS base, providing a minimal, immutable, automatically-updating platform for containerized workloads. It is the server counterpart to the Bazzite / Bluefin / Aurora desktops.
